completed_steps.description: Loaded documentation requirements for 12 project types from CSV
Checked for existing docs (found extensive architecture docs), chose full_rescan mode, initialized state file
Detected project structure: 11 Laravel packages in monorepo, confirmed all packages with composer.json analysis
Discovered existing documentation in /docs/architecture/ (25+ detailed domain docs), assessed coverage
Analyzed technology stack: PHP 8.2+, Laravel 11+, Eloquent ORM, PHPUnit/Pest testing, 42 total migrations
Performed deep scan of all 11 packages: read composer.json, analyzed service providers, models, repositories, events, migrations, test coverage
Generated comprehensive BMAD-style output documents (6 master docs + state file)

findings.dependency_direction: All packages depend on prophoto-contracts (foundation), forms clean DAG
integration_points: Asset pipeline: Upload → Ingest → Session Matching → Assets → Intelligence
Gallery workflow: Booking → Assets → Gallery → Sharing
Event-driven: 14+ events defined, 1+ listeners registered in code
documentation_gaps: Some complex services (SessionMatchingService, IntelligenceExecutionService) lack inline documentation
Event listener registration incomplete in some packages (verify in boot methods)
architectural_strengths: Clean separation of concerns via Laravel packages
known_issues: 7 packages lack unit tests (critical gap for booking, gallery, interactions, invoicing, notifications, access, ai)
Some ServiceProviders may not have event listener registration (ingest, intelligence)
No performance optimization notes in code for large galleries

outputs_generated.description: Master entry point with navigation and system overview
Strategic architecture, patterns, technology stack, data flows
Complete directory structure and critical file locations for all packages
All services, repositories, models, events, contracts, DTOs, enums across packages
Event definitions, interface contracts, DTOs, enums with code examples
Complete database schema, 30+ tables, relationships, migrations
Setup, testing, deployment, common tasks, debugging
This state file - scan metadata and findings summary

critical_gaps: Booking package (workflow engine) has no tests
Gallery package (complex schema, 15 migrations) has no tests

sprint_1.name: Phase 1a — Metadata Extraction + Calendar OAuth
stories: 1a.1 MetadataExtractor.ts
1a.2 CalendarOAuthService + CalendarTokenService
1a.5 DB migrations (upload_sessions, ingest_files, ingest_image_tags)
1a.6 UploadSessionService (15 unit tests)

sprint_2.name: Phase 1a — Calendar Matching + API Endpoint
stories: 1a.3 CalendarMatcherService (22 unit tests)
1a.4 POST /api/ingest/match-calendar (IngestController + routes)
1a.7 UploadInitiator.tsx
1a.8 CalendarMatchDisplay.tsx

sprint_3.name: Phase 1b — Gallery Ingest UI + Tagging
stories: 1b.1 IngestGallery.tsx Inertia page
1b.2 UploadManager.ts + useUploadManager hook
1b.3 TaggingPanel.tsx + UploadStatusBar.tsx
1b.4 registerFiles + uploadFile + applyTag + removeTag + batchUpdateFiles endpoints (11 unit tests)

sprint_4.name: Phase 1b — Charts Tab + Calendar Tab + Upload Manager Polish
stories: IngestEntrypoint.tsx — 3-stage page orchestrator (initiate → match → gallery)
CalendarTab.tsx — session calendar context tab with confidence breakdown + unlink action
ChartsTab ported to new component location (ISO, aperture, focal length, camera, timeline charts)
TaggingPanel updated to 4 tabs: Tags / Session / Charts / Info
Upload retry handler wired: failed files re-enqueued via UploadManager.enqueue()
handleUnlinkCalendar wired through IngestGallery → TaggingPanel → CalendarTab
DELETE /api/ingest/sessions/{id}/unlink-calendar endpoint
GET /ingest Inertia entrypoint (web middleware, renders IngestEntrypoint)
UploadInitiator onMatchComplete signature updated to pass File[] to parent

sprint_5.name: Phase 1c — Backend Event System + Asset Creation
stories: 1c.1 IngestSessionConfirmed event — readonly class dispatched from UploadSessionService::confirmSession()
1c.2 IngestSessionConfirmedListener — iterates uploaded non-culled IngestFiles, creates Assets via AssetCreationService
1c.3 GenerateAssetThumbnail queue job — GD-based JPEG thumbnail, 3 retries + exponential backoff, dispatches AssetDerivativesGenerated
1c.4 GET /api/ingest/sessions/{id}/preview-status endpoint — polls asset creation progress + thumbnails
1c.5 IngestServiceProvider — Event::listen(IngestSessionConfirmed, IngestSessionConfirmedListener) registered in boot()
1c.5 Unit tests — IngestSessionConfirmedEventTest (6 tests) + IngestSessionConfirmedListenerTest (6 tests)

sprint_6.name: Phase 1c — Gallery Context Projection
stories: 1c.6 GalleryContextProjectionListener — bulk-inserts Image records per Asset, chunk(50), idempotent
1c.7 Intelligence Queue — confirmed ALREADY WIRED via IntelligenceServiceProvider listening to AssetReadyV1 (no new code needed)
Migration 2026_04_11_000013 — adds ingest_session_id, ingest_file_id, ingest_tags, calendar_event_id to images table
Image model — added ingest context fields to fillable + ingest_tags cast
GalleryServiceProvider — Event::listen(IngestSessionConfirmed, GalleryContextProjectionListener) registered
8 unit tests in Sprint6/GalleryContextProjectionListenerTest.php

sprint_7.name: Phase 1c — Controller Feature Tests + N+1 Fix + Performance Indexes + Launch
stories: N+1 fix: batchUpdateFiles now uses single whereIn + bulk DB::table update (was per-row query in foreach)
22 HTTP Feature tests in Feature/IngestControllerTest.php covering all 9 JSON endpoints
Migration 2026_04_11_000004: 6 performance indexes (upload_sessions status+studio_status+confirmed_at, ingest_files culled+session_status_culled+rating, ingest_image_tags type_tag)
docs/LAUNCH-CHECKLIST.md: 10-section launch checklist with sign-off table

phase_2a.name: Project-Level PRD Generation
key_content: Problem statement (photographers waste 50 min/shoot on organization)
5 primary strategic goals with measurable outcomes

phase_2b.name: Feature-Level PRD Generation (Phase 1 Epic: Complete Ingest & Post-Upload Workflow)
key_content: Complete Phase 1 ingest workflow specification (not just upload recognition)
User problem: 45-60 min manual file organization per shoot → <5 min automated
3 pillars: metadata-first ingest, calendar as context engine, immediate tagging interface
4 core user workflows with preconditions & steps
3 user personas (Sarah, James, Lisa) with detailed stories

phase_3.name: Architecture Design for Phase 1 Implementation
key_content: High-level data flow (8-step journey from upload to asset creation)
Component architecture: Frontend (metadata extractor, gallery UI, upload manager) + Backend (calendar matcher, session manager, API controllers)
Database schema: 4 new tables (upload_sessions, ingest_files, ingest_image_tags, upload_session_metadata)
5 new API endpoints with request/response specs
Event-driven workflow: SessionAssociationResolved event triggering downstream listeners
Performance targets: <5s metadata extraction, <3s calendar match, <2s gallery render, <100ms filtering
Comprehensive error handling (network failures, corrupt metadata, edge cases)
Code examples (TypeScript metadata extractor, PHP calendar matcher)

next_steps_for_phase_4: Epic User Story Breakdown: Transform ARCH-phase-1-ingest-design.md into implementation-ready user stories/tickets
Engineering Sprint Planning: Schedule weeks 1-9 with capacity planning and dependency sequencing
Design System Handoff: UI component specifications and responsive design specs for ingest gallery
Calendar OAuth Setup: Configure Google Calendar API credentials and testing environment
Database Migrations: Prepare Laravel migration files for upload_sessions, ingest_files, ingest_image_tags, upload_session_metadata
Development Environment: Spin up test environment with S3/Redis/PostgreSQL configured
Reference Implementation Review: Team walkthrough of ingest-pro V0 prototype code
Risk Assessment: Identify potential bottlenecks (concurrent uploads, metadata extraction performance, calendar API rate limits)

resumability.operator_mode: solo - Dave Peloso (no other agents used)
resumability.context_persistence: Full session context maintained by Claude in single conversation thread

recommendations.impact: Workflow engine is critical, currently untested

recommendations.action: Add integration tests for asset→intelligence pipeline
recommendations.impact: Core intelligence generation flow needs coverage

recommendations.action: Add OpenAPI/Swagger documentation for API endpoints
recommendations.impact: Better developer experience and API contract clarity

recommendations.action: Verify event listener registrations in IngestServiceProvider and IntelligenceServiceProvider
recommendations.impact: Ensure event-driven communication is wired correctly

recommendations.action: Profile and optimize SessionMatchingService (critical path in ingest)
recommendations.impact: Matching performance affects user upload experience

recommendations.action: Replace NullAssetMetadataExtractor with real metadata extraction (EXIF, IPTC, XMP)
recommendations.impact: Enables better asset discovery and organization

validation_notes: All 11 packages confirmed present with active composer.json files
Technology stack verified: PHP 8.2+, Laravel 11+, Eloquent, PHPUnit/Pest
Event definitions consistent across packages (using prophoto-contracts/Events)
Service bindings follow Laravel ServiceProvider pattern
Multi-tenancy model consistent (studio_id, organization_id on all key tables)
Migration counts match package complexity (gallery: 15, assets: 6, ingest: 2, etc.)
summary: BMAD Document Project Phases 1-3 completed successfully (225 minutes total, solo operation by Dave Peloso). Phase 1 (Analysis): Deep scan of 11-package Laravel monorepo captured architecture patterns, 28+ models, 22+ services, 14 events, 13 contracts, 42 migrations. Phase 2A (Project-Level PRD): Generated strategic positioning document ($720M market, clear business model, 4-phase roadmap). Phase 2B (Feature-Level PRD): Generated complete Phase 1 epic specification (ingest workflow, 13 P0 requirements, 9-week timeline, success metrics). Phase 3 (Architecture Design): Delivered detailed technical architecture (15 sections, 28KB, component specs, API contracts, database schema, error handling, performance targets, implementation roadmap). Ready for Phase 4 (Epic Breakdown & Sprint Planning).
